wrong_split: مُلْك + ك ("your")
correct: root م-ل-ك, ٱل + مُلْك; final ك is the 3rd radical
reason: root-final radical is not the 2nd-person enclitic ك

wrong_split: مَلِك + ك
correct: root م-ل-ك 'the king'; ك is radical
reason: root-final radical, not a pronoun

wrong_split: noun لَ + هُ possessive
correct: preposition لِ + pronoun هُ = 'for him'
reason: preposition+pronoun, not a possessed noun

wrong_split: noun فَلَ + هُ
correct: فَ + لِ + هُ = 'so for him'
reason: proclitic فـ + preposition + pronoun

wrong_split: noun وَلَ + هُ
correct: وَ + لِ + هُ = 'and for him / and to Him belongs'
reason: و + preposition + pronoun

wrong_split: بيد + ه as opaque stem
correct: بِ + يَد + هِ = 'in His hand'
reason: proclitic بِ + stem يَد + enclitic هِ; segment all three, do not treat بيد as a stem

wrong_split: noun وَأَنّ + ا / + نا
correct: وَ + أَنَّ + نَا = 'and that we' (إنّ-sister + 1pl pronoun)
reason: particle أنّ + pronoun, not a possessed noun + نا

wrong_split: قُرْءَا + نا (pronoun)
correct: قُرْءَان + tanwīn fatḥ (ـًا) = indefinite accusative
reason: tanwīn-alif is not the pronoun نا (ends_tanwin_alef)

wrong_split: بُنْيَٰ + نا
correct: بُنْيَان + tanwīn = 'a structure'
reason: tanwīn-alif, not نا; also بنيان ≠ ابن

wrong_split: إِنسَا + نٌ as suffix-n
correct: root أ-ن-س, إنسان + tanwīn ḍamm; final ن is a radical
reason: root-final ن vs tanwīn-nūn: here it is BOTH radical + case-tanwīn, not an added نون

wrong_split: root د-ع-ع 'shove'
correct: root د-ع-و 'to call/invoke', present pl; نَ is the verbal ending
reason: norm key يدعون collapses د-ع-و and د-ع-ع; QAC root + context = call/invoke

wrong_split: noun عِلْم + نا 'our knowledge'
correct: verb عَلِمَ + نَا (subject) = 'we knew'
reason: verb host: نا is the subject pronoun, not a possessive; POS-gate

wrong_split: (none — genuine)
correct: أَعْمَال (noun pl) + نَا = 'our deeds'
reason: genuine noun stem + possessive enclitic (POSITIVE control)

wrong_split: (none — genuine)
correct: سَيِّئَات (broken pl) + هِمْ = 'their evil deeds'
reason: broken plural shares the root س-و-أ, not the surface; POSITIVE control

wrong_split: رَحْم + ة as ه pronoun
correct: رَحْمَة, the ة is tāʾ marbūṭa (feminine), not the 3ms pronoun ه
reason: bare(ة)≠ه; ة is a feminine marker, not an enclitic

wrong_split: noun فِي + هِ possessive
correct: preposition فِي + pronoun هِ = 'in it'
reason: preposition + pronoun

wrong_split: noun عَلَيْ + هِمْ
correct: preposition عَلَىٰ + هِمْ = 'upon them'
reason: preposition (with alef-maqsura→ya before pronoun) + pronoun

wrong_split: (none — genuine)
correct: إِيمَان (maṣdar) + هِمْ = 'their faith'
reason: genuine maṣdar + possessive; root أ-م-ن (POSITIVE control)

wrong_split: verb 'to send'
correct: رَسُول (noun) + tanwīn = 'a messenger'
reason: noun, not the verb أرسل; POS guard

wrong_split: verb بَنَى 'to build'
correct: ٱبْن (noun) = 'son'
reason: noun ابن/بنات/بني are not the verb 'to build'

wrong_split: host-only سَلَام = 'peace'
correct: بِـ + سَلَام majrūr; public hover must include the bāʾ relation such as 'with peace' or 'in peace' when certified
reason: attached preposition is visible morphology, not hidden metadata

wrong_split: host-only بَدْر = 'Badr'
correct: بِـ + proper noun بَدْر; public hover must include the bāʾ relation such as 'at Badr' when certified
reason: attached preposition teaches the entry sense and cannot be omitted

wrong_split: lemma-only جَادَلَ = 'to argue/dispute'
correct: جَادَلُوا + كَ: plural subject arguing/disputing with second-person masculine singular object
reason: verb suffix pronoun changes the user-visible token gloss

wrong_split: host-only يَعْفُوا = 'pardon'
correct: وَ + لام الأمر + يَعْفُوا: and let them pardon
reason: imperative lām and plural subject are visible token morphology

wrong_split: lemma/root gloss = 'to teach'
correct: passive participle from Form II: taught
reason: passive participle is not a finite infinitive/lemma gloss

wrong_split: lemma-only سأل = 'to ask, question'
correct: يَـ imperfect prefix + سْـَٔلُ host stem + كَ object pronoun = ask you
reason: attached object pronoun and imperfect prefix must be learner-visible; lemma gloss is not a token gloss

wrong_split: fluent phrase with no component proof
correct: فَ + أَهْلَكْنَا + هُمْ = so/then We destroyed them
reason: fā', Form IV verb, subject, and object suffix all need explicit rich-hover breakdown

wrong_split: fallback text 'and + the trees' counted as rich certification
correct: وَ + ٱل + شَّجَرُ = and + the + trees, with segment roles and intact Arabic
reason: plus signs are not a learner explanation; conjunction, article, and host must be separately teachable

wrong_split: host phrase gloss with no bā' component
correct: بِـ + ٱل + مَعْرُوفِ = by/with what is right/customary, pending PP attachment proof
reason: bā' relation and definite host must be visible before rich certification

wrong_split: nominal/root gloss such as 'partners'
correct: تُخَالِطُوا + هُمْ = you mix/associate with them
reason: finite verb plus object suffix cannot be replaced by an entry-family noun gloss

wrong_split: bare verb gloss such as 'to hold'
correct: تُمْسِكُوا + هُنَّ = you hold/keep them (feminine plural)
reason: attached feminine plural object pronoun must be visible to the learner

wrong_split: host-only noun gloss such as 'doors'
correct: أَبْوَٰب + هَا = its doors/gates
reason: noun-host possessive suffix is a positive control distinct from verb-object suffixes

wrong_split: bare verb gloss such as 'to write'
correct: تَكْتُبُوا + هَا = you write it/her
reason: object suffix must be visible, and the exact it/her referent stays gated

wrong_split: host-only ذُنُوب = 'Sin.' or 'sins'
correct: بِـ + ذُنُوب + هِمْ = because of/for their sins when the causal/prepositional relation is certified
reason: bā' and the possessive suffix both change the learner-visible token contribution

wrong_split: generic suffix pending with no bā' lane
correct: بِـ + ذُنُوب + كُم = because of/for your sins when certified
reason: the row needs preposition plus possessed-host review, not only suffix review

wrong_split: host-only رُوح = 'spirit'
correct: بِـ + رُوح; bā' relation and referent must be visible or explicitly pending
reason: host-only spirit omits the governed preposition and may hide a referent decision

wrong_split: host-only طُور = 'Mount'
correct: وَ + طُور = and by Mount when oath reading is certified
reason: oath/coordinating wāw governs the host contribution and cannot vanish

wrong_split: bare demonstrative 'this'
correct: وَ + هَٰذَا = and by this / and this according to the certified function
reason: demonstrative host text is not enough when the particle function changes the token contribution

wrong_split: host-only بَابِل = 'Babylon'
correct: بِـ + بَابِلَ = in Babylon when locative bā' is certified
reason: a place-name host still needs the attached preposition relation

wrong_split: يَا + بِسٍ treated as a vocative because of raw prefix
correct: يَابِسٍ = one lexical host meaning dry
reason: the initial yā belongs to the lexical stem; vocative routing requires real segmentation evidence

wrong_split: يَا + بِسَاتٍ treated as vocative/addressee structure
correct: يَابِسَٰتٍ = one lexical feminine plural host meaning dry things
reason: surface-prefix matching would create a fake vocative; sarf must keep the lexical stem intact

wrong_split: بُـ + كْمٌ treated as an attached bā' preposition candidate from raw first letter
correct: بُكْمٌ is one lexical host meaning mute/dumb; no attached bā' is certified
reason: preposition routing requires real token-internal segmentation, not a bā'-like first letter

wrong_split: لِـ + سَانِ treated as a lām preposition plus host from raw first letter
correct: لِسَانِ is one lexical host meaning tongue/language; no attached lām is certified
reason: the initial lām belongs to the lexical stem; lām-jarr/amr routing needs positive segmentation evidence

wrong_split: فَـ + الِقُ treated as a result/conjunction fā' from raw first letter
correct: فَالِقُ is one lexical active-participle host meaning splitter/cleaver; no attached fā' is certified
reason: fā' particle routing requires a real proclitic segment; lexical fā' must stay with the host

correct: فَأَهْلَكْنَاهُمْ = فَـ + Form IV verb host + هُمْ object pronoun; component entries are not whole-token candidates
reason: component candidates can teach segmentation, but they must not become the whole-token hover or parse-family certificate
wrong_split: فَـ or هُمْ entry treated as the whole token

correct: يَسْـَٔلُكَ = imperfect verb host + كَ object pronoun
reason: the attached kāf is visible object morphology and cannot be hidden behind a lemma gloss
wrong_split: bare verb 'ask' or pronoun-only كَ whole-token hover

correct: لَيْتَنِي = لَيْتَ + نِي first-person suffix contribution
reason: a wishing particle with an attached first-person suffix is not the bare particle alone
wrong_split: bare لَيْتَ hover 'it is wished that' with no I contribution

correct: لِكَيْلَا = لِـ + كَيْ + لَا with purpose, governor, and negation roles
reason: composite particles must preserve every visible function piece and governed mood
wrong_split: bare كَيْ hover 'so that' with negation hidden

correct: لَكُنَّا is not the contrast particle لَكِنْ; damma on kāf selects a verb construction
reason: norm_strict equality cannot certify a particle when vocalized surface and POS differ
wrong_split: لَكُنَّا routed to لَكِنْ particle because the consonant key matches

correct: نِعْمَ is a praise/fixed verb reading, not the response particle نَعَمْ
reason: content-letter kasra/fatha distinguishes POS and function
wrong_split: نِعْمَ inherits نَعَمْ 'yes' from a collapsed key

correct: ظَلَمُونَا = finite plural verb + نَا object contribution
reason: VN-00 found finite verb hovers where the attached object pronoun disappeared into an infinitive/root gloss
wrong_split: bare verb or infinitive hover with no object pronoun

correct: وَيُرِيكُمْ = وَ + Form IV finite verb + كُمْ object contribution
reason: wāw function, derived form, and object suffix must all be visible or explicitly gated
wrong_split: root-family gloss such as 'to see/show' with no you-object

correct: شَيَٰطِينِهِمْ = plural host + هِمْ possessive suffix
reason: a plural noun with a possessive suffix cannot be represented by concept/etymon prose alone
wrong_split: Satan/devil concept spread with no plural or their-possessor

correct: تُبَٰشِرُوهُنَّ = finite verb host + هُنَّ feminine plural object pronoun
reason: VN-01 found a contextual phrase hover where the visible object suffix was not available as parse proof
wrong_split: contextual phrase or lemma hover with no feminine-plural object suffix

correct: تَجِدُوهُ = finite verb host + هُ object pronoun
reason: a visible هُ object suffix cannot disappear behind the lemma gloss 'to find'
wrong_split: bare infinitive hover 'to find' with no object

correct: أُجُورَكُمْ = plural noun host + كُمْ possessive suffix
reason: VN-01 found a noun-host token under a verb/root-family entry; the possessive suffix and noun POS must stay visible
wrong_split: root-family reward prose with no 'your' contribution

correct: دِهَاقًۭا has tanwīn/case ending, not an attached pronoun
reason: VN-01 detector noise showed suffix risk on tanwīn/case endings; no pronoun exists to preserve
wrong_split: final case/tanwīn treated as a suffix pronoun

correct: وَفَضَّلْنَٰهُمْ = وَ + Form II perfect 1cp verb + هُمْ object pronoun
reason: VN-02 found broad favor/bounty entry prose on a finite verb whose subject and object are visible morphology
wrong_split: root-family favor prose with no We/them contribution

correct: فُضِّلُوا۟ is passive Form II perfect 3mp, not active 'to favor'
reason: VN-02 found passive voice erased by active dictionary prose
wrong_split: active/root infinitive hover 'to favor' or favor-family paragraph

correct: ٱسْتَـْٔذَنَكَ = hamzated Form X perfect verb + كَ object pronoun
reason: VN-02 found a Form X hamzated verb where the attached kāf must not vanish into lemma prose
wrong_split: bare 'to seek permission' with no you-object

correct: أَصْبَرَهُمْ has a hamza/Form IV or exclamatory shape plus هُمْ suffix; it is not plain صبر
reason: VN-02 found Form I patience prose on a token whose prefix and suffix require exact construction review
wrong_split: plain 'to be patient' hover with no construction or object suffix

correct: عَقَلُوهُ = perfect plural verb host + هُ object pronoun
reason: VN-03 found a bare 'to reason; understand' hover on a finite verb whose attached object suffix must remain visible
wrong_split: bare infinitive hover with no object suffix

correct: وَيَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ = وَ + finite verb host + كَ object pronoun
expected_visible_gloss: and they ask you to hasten
and they seek to hasten you
reason: VN-03 found component-only 'to hasten' evidence on a written token whose wāw and kāf must be accounted for
wrong_split: bare 'to hasten' with no wāw function or you-object

correct: أَعْجَلَكَ = finite verb host + كَ object pronoun
reason: VN-03 found a bare hasten-family gloss on a finite token with attached كَ object
wrong_split: bare 'to hasten' with no object suffix

correct: زِينَتَهُنَّ = nominal host + هُنَّ possessive suffix
reason: VN-03 found possessed nominal rows inside a verb/root family; the suffix must remain visible even when the English string is plausible
wrong_split: adornment host or verb-family prose with no feminine-plural possessor

correct: فَأَنسَىٰهُ = فَ + finite weak/causative verb + هُ object pronoun
expected_visible_gloss: then he caused him to forget
reason: VN-04 found component-only ن س ي evidence leaking a dictionary infinitive onto a finite token with a visible suffix
wrong_split: bare 'to forget; neglect' with no fā' relation or object suffix

correct: ذَرْهُمْ = imperative weak verb host + هُمْ object pronoun
reason: VN-04 found weak imperative/object rows receiving the entry infinitive
wrong_split: bare 'to leave someone or something' without the them-object

correct: ذُكِرَ is a passive finite verb surface, not the noun ذَكَر or a broad infinitive
reason: VN-04 found passive ذ ك ر rows routed through noun/root-family evidence
wrong_split: male noun or active/root infinitive hover

correct: يُوصِيكُمُ is a finite verb with كُم object, not a bare will/instruct entry gloss
reason: VN-05 found a token-only override where كُم vanished behind entry infinitive prose
wrong_split: entry infinitive 'to instruct...' with no you-object

correct: تَبَرُّوهُمْ is a finite verb with هُمْ object, not root-family kindness prose
reason: VN-05 found object suffix loss on a finite verb in the ب ر ر family
wrong_split: broad 'to act kindly, vast in grace' with no them-object

correct: فَسَيَكْفِيكَهُمُ must preserve فَ, future/verb host, كَ object, and هُمُ object stack
expected_visible_gloss: then He will suffice you against them
so He will be sufficient for you against them
reason: VN-05 found stacked pronouns hidden behind 'to be sufficient'
wrong_split: bare 'to be sufficient' with no pronoun stack

correct: لَّازِبٍ has lexical root-initial lām; there is no detachable lām particle
reason: VN-05 found false lām-preposition attachment on a lexical adjective
wrong_split: initial ل treated as preposition/purpose/oath lām

correct: وَأَرْجُلَكُم is component evidence until وَ + host + كُم are certified as one token
expected_visible_gloss: pending: and your feet/legs by exact context
reason: VN-05 found component-only noun evidence that must not certify the whole token
wrong_split: component candidate 'legs' treated as whole-token hover

correct: مِن is a preposition/function token, not a segment of the verb مَنَّ
reason: VN-06 found مِن rows in a م ن ن verb-entry tranche; source-key candidate evidence cannot certify a whole-token verb hover
wrong_split: مِن treated as a مَنَّ verb-entry row or parse-family proof

correct: مَنْ is a function token whose role is relative/interrogative/conditional by context
reason: VN-06 found مَنْ rows pulled through a verb-entry candidate; harakah and context decide the function
wrong_split: مَنْ merged with مِن or verb مَنَّ by stripped surface/root

correct: حَوْلَهُۥ = lexical/adverbial host + هُ suffix, not host-only around
reason: VN-06 found possessed/adverbial noun rows in a verb-entry family; the attached هُ cannot disappear
wrong_split: host-only 'around' or verb-entry candidate with no suffix contribution

correct: ثَمَرِهِۦ = noun host + هِ possessor, not the verb أَثْمَرَ
reason: VN-06 found fruit noun rows in a verb-entry tranche; suffix and noun role remain visible gates
wrong_split: verb 'to bear fruit' or host-only fruit with no possessor

correct: مِنِّى = preposition مِن + 1st person suffix pronoun, not a تَمَنَّى verb row

reason: VN-07 found finite wish-family rows whose object suffix can disappear behind entry infinitive prose

reason: VN-08 found component-only waiting-family evidence that must not certify a whole written token

expected_visible_gloss: they bargain/pledge with you by context
reason: VN-08 found pledge-family entry prose hiding the كَ object suffix
